records.ety: [OE. welthe, from wele; cf. D. weelde luxury. See Weal prosperity.]

defs: Large possessions; a comparative abundance of things which are objects of human desire; esp., abundance of worldly estate; affluence; opulence; riches.
quotes: I have little wealth to lose.
Each day new wealth, without their care, provides.
Wealth comprises all articles of value and nothing else.

defs: In the private sense, all property which has a money value.
In the public sense, all objects, esp. material objects, which have economic utility.
Those energies, faculties, and habits directly contributing to make people industrially efficient; in this sense, specifically called personal wealth.
